one_line_takeaway: A 2B dual-model (content + jailbreak) LLM guardrail that, via bilingual data curation (CHT/BHCB) and a priority-switching curriculum (PSNI), matches or beats 8B-12B guardrails on English/Korean safety benchmarks at ~6.4 GB GPU memory, but its strongest evidence rests on proprietary benchmarks and requires both components running together.
key_finding: The 2B SGuard-v1 matches or beats substantially larger guardrail baselines. On content safety (Table 2, F1/AUPRC/pAUROC average) ContentFilter-2B scores 0.83/0.91/0.88 vs Llama-Guard-4-12B 0.62/0.78/0.73 and Qwen3Guard-Gen-4B 0.79/0.92/0.86. On English jailbreak detection (Table 3 average F1/FNR/FPR) JailbreakFilter-2B reaches 0.85/0.18/0.17 with 0.01 benign FPR, versus AWS Bedrock 0.65/0.30/0.63 (0.08 benign FPR) and Kanana-Safeguard-Prompt-2.1B 0.67/0.38/0.18. The two components are complementary: in English red teaming (Table 6, ASR%) ContentFilter alone leaves Type-3 attacks at 91.3% ASR, but ContentFilter+JailbreakFilter jointly (SGuard-v1 2+2B) cuts Type-3 to 7.9% and Type-2 to 0.2%; in Korean (Table 7) Type-3 drops from 99.6% (ContentFilter alone) to 0.5% jointly. It is also far lighter: 6,357 MB GPU memory vs 24,033 MB (Llama-Guard-4-12B) and 16,353 MB (Kanana-Safeguard-8B). Released under Apache-2.0.
core_contribution: SGuard-v1 is a lightweight, bilingual (English/Korean) safety guardrail system for LLMs from Samsung SDS, built on the 2B-parameter Granite-3.3-2B-Instruct base model. It consists of two separately trained components: (1) ContentFilter, which classifies safety risks in both user prompts and model responses into five consolidated categories, and (2) JailbreakFilter, which detects adversarial/jailbreak prompts as a safe-unsafe binary decision with confidence scores. The paper's own contributions are three novel data/training techniques: CHT (Contextual Harm Translation) to produce faithful Korean translations of harmful text by bypassing translator safety alignment via a 70B refiner; BHCB (Benign-Harmful Contextual Blending) data augmentation to synthesize new unsafe examples and balance the corpus; and PSNI (Priority Switching with Noise Injection), a two-phase curriculum that alternates prompt emphasis between safety and helpfulness to reduce overfitting and false positives on jailbreak detection. About 1.4M total training instances were curated and split across the two components.
threat_model: External input/output guardrail for human-AI conversational deployments. The adversary submits harmful prompts or, more importantly, adversarial jailbreak prompts (60 major attack types, e.g. DAN-style templates, prompt injection, obfuscation) intended to bypass an LLM's built-in safety alignment and elicit illegal/biased/unethical outputs. The guardrail operates as an external policy-enforcement layer that does not modify model weights, screening both inputs (ContentFilter + JailbreakFilter) and generated outputs (ContentFilter). A key stated defensive concern is false-unsafe (false-positive) misclassification that would harm legitimate use; the design explicitly trades this off. Assumed limitations: no reliable coverage outside English/Korean (vulnerable to low-resource-language attacks), recommended input up to 8k tokens, and only training-defined risks are detectable.
limitations: Authors' stated limits: no 100% accuracy, with degraded detection and higher FPR near the harmfulness decision boundary and under novel/unseen attack techniques; taxonomy tuned to common international use cases so misclassification rises in highly specialized domains; performance only guaranteed for English and Korean despite the base model's 12-language support, with acknowledged vulnerability to low-resource-language attacks; not a conversational model (no dialogue continuation), recommended input <=8k tokens; only detects training-defined risks. Reviewer-observed gaps: heavy reliance on undisclosed proprietary datasets/red-teaming and a proprietary Korean benchmark limits reproducibility of the strongest claims; ContentFilter alone is weak against sophisticated jailbreaks (Table 6/7 Type-3 >91%), so security depends on running both models; label quality rests on a single unspecified 70B LLM re-labeler; baseline tables have several missing cells (Kanana AUPRC/pAUROC); no statistical significance or variance reported; the striking joint-ASR reductions come only from the authors' own internal red-teaming framework, not a public adversarial benchmark.

datasets_used: WildGuardMix (86,759 English safe/unsafe seed examples for ContentFilter)
Aegis / Aegis2.0 (30,007 English seed examples for ContentFilter)
MLCommons AILuminate hazard taxonomy (12 categories, consolidated to 5)
~1M uncurated jailbreak/benign prompts (JailbreakFilter phase 1; sources incl. Jailbreak-PromptBank, Catch-the-prompt-injection, WildTeaming, GuardrailsAI)
60-attack-type jailbreak seeds from Shen et al. 2024, Liu et al. 2024, Yu et al. 2023 (GPTFuzzer)
BeaverTails, HarmfulQA, OpenAI Moderation, ToxicChat, XSTest (content-safety eval)
Proprietary Korean content-safety, proprietary jailbreak, and internal 3-tier red-teaming datasets
quantitative_results: Base model: Granite-3.3-2B-Instruct (2B params, 12 languages supported; trained/eval for EN+KO)
Training scale: ~1.4M total instances; ContentFilter ~500K acquired -> 400K trained (1 epoch, 5-way NLL, LR 3e-5); JailbreakFilter 1M phase-1 + 5.4K phase-2 (LR 1e-5)
Content safety avg F1/AUPRC/pAUROC (Table 2): SGuard-ContentFilter-2B 0.83/0.91/0.88; Llama-Guard-4-12B 0.62/0.78/0.73; Kanana-Safeguard-8B 0.76/-/-; Qwen3Guard-Gen-4B 0.79/0.92/0.86

English jailbreak avg F1/FNR/FPR (Table 3): SGuard-JailbreakFilter-2B 0.85/0.18/0.17 (benign FPR 0.01); AWS Bedrock 0.65/0.30/0.63 (0.08); Azure AI Content Safety 0.58/0.57/0.04 (0.01); Kanana-Safeguard-Prompt-2.1B 0.67/0.38/0.18 (0.01)
Korean jailbreak avg F1/FNR/FPR (Table 4): SGuard 0.86/0.19/0.08 (benign 0.01); AWS 0.64/0.34/0.54; Azure 0.49/0.64/0.06; Kanana-Prompt-2.1B 0.63/0.40/0.28
Proprietary Korean content safety F1/AUPRC/pAUROC (Table 5): SGuard-ContentFilter-2B 0.900/0.969/0.886; LlamaGuard-4-12B 0.827/0.938/0.837; Kanana-Safeguard-8B 0.896/-/-
English red-team ASR% Type1/2/3 (Table 6): WildGuard-7B 9.5/11.7/25.7; SGuard-ContentFilter-2B 8.5/13.8/91.3; SGuard-v1 (2+2B) 8.5/0.2/7.9
Korean red-team ASR% Type1/2/3 (Table 7): Kanana-Safeguard(8+8+2.1B) 18.3/7.8/99.7; SGuard-ContentFilter-2B 7.3/30.1/99.6; SGuard-v1 (2+2B) 7.3/1.3/0.5
GPU memory on H100 (Table 8): SGuard-ContentFilter-2B 6,357 MB; Qwen3Guard-Gen-4B 9,177 MB; WildGuard-7B 14,787 MB; Kanana-Safeguard-8B 16,353 MB; Llama-Guard-4-12B 24,033 MB
PSNI noise hyperparameters: alpha=0.1, beta=0.02 default (0 < alpha,beta <= 0.1)
